prompt: Fix the JSON path query that fails on nested arrays

turns.content:
<task>
Fix the JSON path query function — $.users[0].name works but $.users[*].name (wildcard) returns empty array.
</task>

arguments.reasoning: Line 16: when index is '*', it does `next.push(arr)` — this pushes the entire array as a single element instead of spreading its items. Should be `next.push(...arr)` to push each array element individually.

turns.content:
PASS src/utils/json-query.test.ts
  jsonQuery

Tests: 5 passed, 5 total

arguments.summary: Fixed JSON path wildcard in src/utils/json-query.ts:16. Was pushing entire array as single element (`next.push(arr)`) instead of spreading items (`next.push(...arr)`). Wildcard [*] now correctly expands all array elements. Found file via glob('**/query*'). All 5 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed JSON path wildcard query. Changed push(arr) to push(...arr) to spread array elements instead of nesting them. All 5 tests pass.
